one_liner: DE down 2.8% on high distribution volume with RSI at 67 (near overbought) and DCF showing 68% downside to $199 fair value — near 90-day resistance at $619; the agricultural equipment cycle is turning.

news_catalyst.headline: Deere & Company Pulling Back Near 90-Day Resistance — Agricultural Cycle Softening
news_catalyst.summary: DE is down 2.8% on 1.55x volume flagged as 'High-volume down day — distribution.' The stock is within 1% of its 90-day resistance at $618.96. Negative revenue growth (a DCF flag) suggests the agricultural equipment supercycle is softening as farmer income normalizes from post-pandemic highs. DCF shows $199 base intrinsic value against a $613 price — the stock reflects peak-cycle earnings, not normalized earnings.

technical.summary: DE is testing resistance at $619 and pulling back on distribution volume. RSI at 67 is near overbought — a rejection at resistance on high sell volume is a classic topping signal. MACD remains bullish but the distribution pattern is concerning. Support at $523 is 14.6% lower.

valuation.summary: 68% DCF downside to base case of $199 — the stock is pricing in perpetual peak-cycle earnings. At $613, investors are paying significantly more than the business's normalized earnings power. Bull case of $454 is still 26% below current price.

key_strengths: Precision agriculture technology (John Deere Operations Center)
key_concerns: Negative revenue growth — agricultural cycle declining
fundamentals.summary: Grade D fundamentals driven by high leverage and negative revenue growth. Deere is a great business but a terrible stock at current prices. The agricultural equipment supercycle peaked in 2022-2024 and the cycle is now reversing.

risks: 68% DCF downside — priced for peak cycle earnings that are now declining
High-volume distribution at 90-day resistance — institutional selling
Negative revenue growth confirmed — agricultural cycle reversal
High leverage (D/E > 3) limits financial flexibility in a downcycle
